fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to hawaii, speech-language pathologist compact hawaii, hawaii speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA)
board.mailing_address:
Professional and Vocational Licensing Division
DCCA, PVL
P. O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Hawaii.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Obtain a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology or its equivalent from an accredited institution. Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your university to the Hawaii Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a minimum of 36 weeks of full-time (or equivalent part-time) supervised professional experience and at least 300 hours of supervised clinical experience (Clinical Fellowship). Have your supervisor submit verification directly to the Board.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score of 162 or higher on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology. Request that your official scores be sent directly from ETS to the Hawaii Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Submit Online Application via MyPVL
steps.description: Create an account or log in to the MyPVL portal, complete the online application for a Speech Pathologist license, and pay the non-refundable application fee ($50) and initial license fee ($50).

steps.title: Provide Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Ensure all required supporting documents, including official transcripts, Praxis scores, and verification of supervised experience, are sent directly to the Board from the respective institutions or supervisors. Monitor your application status within your MyPVL account.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Hawaii permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Hawaii is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Hawaii SLP license is required to treat clients in the state.
provisional_text: Hawaii issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Hawaii does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Hawaii licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $175. Biennial renewal is $120.

faqs.question: Is Hawaii part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: No. Hawaii has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hawaii permits telepractice but requires a valid Hawaii SLP license.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Hawaii does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Hawaii licensure.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Hawaii
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Hawaii, you must submit your application and supporting documents through the state's online licensing portal, MyPVL.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Go to the MyPVL portal and click 'New User Registration'. 2. Create an account by providing your personal information and setting up a username and password. 3. Log in to your new account. 4. Select 'Apply for a License' from the main menu. 5. Choose 'Speech Pathology and Audiology' as the Board/Commission and 'Speech Pathologist' as the profession to begin your application.
